summary: `temper check <harness-root>` silently half-gates: an explicit path arg finds no lock, so declared requirements/satisfies/edges vanish while built-ins still resolve — exit 0, looks green

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: Fix the `check`/`guard` argument-to-workspace resolution (main.rs:39-41 DEFAULT_WORKSPACE): an explicit `<arg>` at a harness root must resolve `<arg>/.temper` the way the bare-verb default (`./.temper`) does, or fail loud — never resolve to a lockless workspace that silently drops the declared model (`drift::read_declarations` finds no lock).

`SESSION_START_COMMAND` (install.rs:88) hardcodes `temper check .` into every adopted harness's SessionStart hook — the half-gate reporter ships on every install. Retarget it to the corrected arg semantics (or `.temper`).

tests.asserts: the install-wired SessionStart command gates the full declared model (a required, unfilled requirement fails) rather than exiting 0 on built-ins alone
`check <harness-root>` resolves the nested `.temper` (or fails loud), never silently half-gates on a lockless workspace

acceptance: `temper check <harness-root>` and the install-wired SessionStart command gate the full declared model (requirements, satisfies fills, custom kinds, edges) or fail loud; no argument spelling produces a silent green on a half-resolved workspace.
notes: Verified at HEAD: install.rs:88 hardcodes `temper check . --reporter session-start`; main.rs:41 DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=`./.temper`, so bare `check` resolves but explicit `.` doesn't. Field-confirmed: `check .` stayed green with a `required:true` requirement unfilled; `check .temper` exits 1. Repo + example hooks hand-fixed (aba7e47, 549969f); the fix is install wiring + arg semantics; guard shares the arg shape. scoped at 01625fb.

summary: Admit the glob-validity predicate family (globs parse under globset, brace-aware); first consumers the rule and skill default contracts over their `paths` fields

edit.description: Add the glob-validity `Predicate` enum variant plus its schema surface — the globs a selected field carries must parse under `globset` (brace-expansion aware, the one glob engine already inside `ignore`). Ordinary clause mechanics: dialable severity, guidance, cite.

Add the glob-validity clause to the `rule` and `skill` default contracts over their `paths` fields, with a fresh raw cite (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-15) — the strictest-documented-profile stance: an unparseable `[` makes the pattern invalid and it silently matches nothing, so the rule never fires / the skill never registers.

Re-derive the frozen built-in lock — the two new default-contract clauses become declaration rows. tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs re-derives from the SDK module and byte-compares, so this file must match that emit.

tests.asserts: a rule whose `paths` carries an unparseable glob (`[`) fires the glob-validity finding at the clause's severity; a valid brace-expansion glob passes
the embedded built-in lock byte-equals the SDK module's memberless emit after the rule/skill default contracts gain the glob-validity clause

acceptance: A rule or skill whose `paths` carries a glob invalid under globset produces a finding at the clause's declared severity; a valid glob (brace-expansion included) passes; the frozen built-in lock re-derives and byte-matches the SDK module's emit.
notes: From decision 0022 (spec delta f67303c, human-ruled 07-15; its work order routes through the inbox). The predicate vocabulary is closed — adding one is a deliberate language change (contract.md clause). The two deferred skill-shape predicates (name hyphen-position, no-XML-in-description) are NOT in scope; `tools-must-resolve` was rejected permanently on invariant 2 (0022) — do not re-file. scoped at 01625fb.

summary: `emit --into <dir>` inside an adopted harness re-roots the projection tree, orphaning live projections the byte-faithful reap then deletes — emit must refuse the reap wave

edit.description: In the emit reap path (`reap_or_report_orphan` / `emit_program`), guard the mass-reap an `--into` re-root triggers: when the target re-roots an already-adopted harness, every prior projection reads as ownerless. Refuse (or dry-run-report) the whole wave instead of deleting live projections byte-faithfully.

The `--into` argument (Emit command, main.rs:39/319): carry whatever signal the reap guard needs to detect a re-root of an adopted harness (e.g. an existing lock at the ambient root the `--into` diverges from). Shares the arg/workspace resolution CHECK-ARG-HALF-GATE corrects — hence blockedBy it.

tests.asserts: `emit --into <nested-dir>` inside an adopted harness refuses (or dry-run-reports) rather than reaping the repo-root projections; plain `emit` still reaps a genuinely single ownerless projection

acceptance: An `--into` that re-roots an already-adopted harness no longer silently deletes live projections — emit refuses or dry-run-reports the reap wave — while a genuine single-orphan reap still works.
notes: Observed at aba7e47: `emit --into .temper` reaped 7 live root projections (CLAUDE.md, five rules, a skill); restored via git, plain `emit` then clean 0/8/0. The prior reap fix (e7b859a) covered path spelling, not re-rooting. Loud-or-nothing (intent 6/7): emit never silently deletes. Shares main.rs's arg/workspace resolution (line 39) with CHECK-ARG — hence blockedBy it. scoped at 01625fb.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
